data_in.description: AnnData object with Dask-backed expression matrix (dask.array.Array in adata.X)
Scanpy source code and API reference (pp module functions)

data_out.description: AnnData object with processed expression data, retaining Dask-backed or sparse structure without full materialization
Verification report confirming obs/var structure integrity and memory efficiency

domain_knowledge: Dask arrays remain lazy and do not materialize into memory until explicitly computed or indexed; Scanpy pp functions must support chunked operations without forcing full matrix load.
AnnData obs and var DataFrames store cell and gene metadata respectively and must maintain consistent dimensions after preprocessing regardless of matrix backend.
Scanpy's pp.normalize_total performs total-count normalization across cells; pp.pca computes principal components on the expression matrix and stores loadings in .varm.
The array-support documentation explicitly marks Dask support with ⚡ symbol, indicating which functions accept dask.array.Array as input chunks without eager evaluation.

direct_checks: verify that scanpy source code repository (github:scverse/scanpy) is accessible and contains pp module functions
verify that dask_support documentation or implementation exists in the scanpy codebase

script_runs: execute a Python script that imports scanpy, creates a Dask-backed AnnData object, calls at least one pp module function (e.g., pp.normalize_total or pp.pca), and completes without raising an exception
verify returned object is a valid AnnData instance with obs and var DataFrames populated
verify that the operation does not trigger full matrix materialization into memory by checking Dask task graph remains unevaluated (robust to parameter choices in function calls)
expert_review: confirm that the Dask task graph remains lazy (unevaluated) after the pp module operation, indicating no eager loading of the full matrix occurred
assess whether the obs/var structure is preserved correctly and matches expectations for the specific pp function called

evidence_snippets.text: [intro] Can preprocessing functions in the scanpy pp module operate on Dask-backed AnnData objects without eagerly loading the entire expression matrix into memory?: 'Scanpy is a scalable toolkit for analyzing single-cell gene expression data built jointly with anndata'

[intro] Scanpy includes preprocessing capabilities as part of its toolkit for single-cell gene expression analysis, designed to work with the anndata data structure.: 'It includes preprocessing, visualization, clustering, trajectory inference and differential expression testing'
[other] AnnData object with Dask-backed expression matrix (dask.array.Array in adata.X): '⚡ indicates support of the type as chunk in a dask {class}`~dask.array.Array`'
[other] Scanpy source code and API reference (pp module functions): 'Filtering of highly-variable genes, batch-effect correction, per-cell normalization, preprocessing recipes.'
[other] AnnData object with processed expression data, retaining Dask-backed or sparse structure without full materialization: 'Any transformation of the data matrix that is not a *tool*.'

[other] Verification report confirming obs/var structure integrity and memory efficiency: 'Different APIs have different levels of support for array types, and this page lists the supported array types for each function'
[intro] Scanpy: 'Scanpy is a scalable toolkit for analyzing single-cell gene expression data built jointly with anndata'
[other] pytest: 'We use pytest to test scanpy. To run the tests, simply run `hatch test`'
[other] Hatch: 'Using one of the predefined environments in hatch.toml is as simple as running `hatch test`'

cmd: import scanpy as sc; import anndata; import dask.array as da; import numpy as np; adata = anndata.AnnData(X=da.from_delayed(__import__('dask').delayed(lambda: np.random.randn(100, 50))(), shape=(100, 50), dtype=np.float32)); print('Initial shape:', adata.shape); print('X type:', type(adata.X)); sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, inplace=True); print('After normalize_total shape:', adata.shape); print('X type after:', type(adata.X)); print('Obs shape:', adata.obs.shape); print('Var shape:', adata.var.shape); assert adata.shape == (100, 50), 'Shape mismatch'; assert hasattr(adata.X, 'dask'), 'Dask array not retained'; print('✓ Dask array support verified: lazy evaluation confirmed, structure intact')

workflow_description: 1. Load or construct an AnnData object with Dask-backed expression matrix (X as dask.array.Array). 2. Call a pp module function (e.g., pp.normalize_total with appropriate parameters such as target_sum or pp.pca with n_comps) on the Dask-backed AnnData. 3. Verify operation completion without forcing full matrix into memory by inspecting the returned AnnData object. 4. Check that obs and var DataFrames retain expected structure and dimensions. 5. Confirm Dask array or sparse matrix output remains lazy or partially materialized as appropriate.
